title: Understand: Separation of Powers: Key Thinkers
description: Check your understanding of the key thinkers associated with the concept of separation of powers with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Understand: Separation of Powers: Key Thinkers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who wrote *The Spirit of the Laws*, which described the importance of the separation of powers?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: James Madison
Montesquieu
Machiavelli
John Locke

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Montesquieu, what happens when the legislative and executive powers are united in the same person or a single group of people?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This is an ideal form of government.
The executive has great power to help the people.
There can be no liberty.
The people will mistrust the judiciary branch.

assessment_items.question_content:
**James Madison outlined his explanation and defense of the Constitution’s separation of powers in which document?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Declaration of Independence
The Virginia Declaration of Rights
Federalist No. 51
*The Spirit of Laws*

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to James Madison, in a good government, “\_\_\_\_ must be made to counteract ambition.”**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a powerful police force
ambition
fair election processes
delays

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is a famous quotation by James Madison? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: "Greed, for lack of a better word, is good."
"If men were angels, no government would be necessary."
"A republic, if you can keep it."
"A house divided against itself cannot stand."

assessment_items.question_content:
**George Washington delivered his Newburgh Address in response to what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A series of letters proposing mutiny and desertion
A pamphlet calling for rebellion against the British government
An act of violence by British soldiers against colonial protests
A secret plan to sabotage the British tea industry

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did colonial soldiers threaten to mutiny during the Revolutionary War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They weren’t receiving their pay.
They didn’t trust George Washington.
They were secretly Loyalists.
They wanted to declare independence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the response to George Washington’s Newburgh Address?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It inspired soldiers to recommit to America.
It encouraged colonists to surrender to the British government.
It ended the Revolutionary War as the British surrendered.
It provided the structure of the federal government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to John Adams, why is a republic the best form of government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is a representative government  based on the rule of law, which protects against tyranny.
It gives the government the most power to ensure law and order.
It prioritizes speed over caution and deliberation, allowing laws to be passed quickly.
It is based on a divine right to rule, not on the preferences of uneducated people.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Although he was in favor of a republic form of government, John Adams wrote that a republican government must \_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: further divide political power
grant the people direct say over policy
concentrate all power in the legislature
guarantee that every person has the right to vote

assessment_items.question_content:
**What were John Adams’s recommendations for the judiciary?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It must be independent and able to check the other branches.
It must be elected by the people.
Judges should serve for short terms of office.
Judges should be subject to recall by the people to prevent judicial overreach.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the influence that Montesquieu had on the founders?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He argued for a strong, centralized monarchy, which influenced the position of president and the organization of the executive branch.
He argued that a system of checks and balances works only in a direct democracy where all citizens can vote on every issue.
He argued for the creation of a confederal system of government in which states held more power than the federal government.
He argued that the power of the government should be divided among different departments, which influenced the structure of the U.S. government.
